channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NDA's Efforts to Connect with Vijay's TVK Face Hurdles Amid Rajinikanth's Influence in Tamil Nadu Elections
item.description: In the upcoming Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, the NDA's attempts to gain support from popular actor Vijay's political front, the 'Vijay TVK', have encountered unexpected difficulties. The primary rea...

item.title: South African Boxer Baleni and Trainer Jack Return Home Following Travel Disruptions in Asia
item.description: South African boxer Siphamandla Baleni and his trainer Khangelani Jack have safely returned home to KuGompo City after being stranded in Asia for over a week due to disruptions in commercial flights c...

item.title: Champlain Hudson Power Express Will Deliver Canadian Hydropower to New York City Homes
item.description: The Champlain Hudson Power Express, a $6 billion, 339-mile underground and underwater transmission line, is set to bring hydropower from Hydro-Quebec in Canada to New York City. Once operational, the ...

item.title: Afghanistan reports hospital hit in alleged airstrike causing hundreds of deaths
item.description: Afghan authorities have reported that a hospital was struck by an airstrike, allegedly carried out by Pakistan, resulting in at least 400 fatalities. The incident has drawn international concern due t...

item.title: Corporate Olympics to Promote Teamwork, Fitness, and Innovation in Nigerian Workplaces
item.description: Na big event dey come for Lagos wey go join business, wellness, and competitive spirit for one place. The Corporate Olympics go happen for Landmark Event Centre and e go gather professionals from top ...

item.title: Today’s Iftar and Tomorrow’s Sehri Timings Across Pakistan
item.description: Dawn has published the daily Iftar and Sehri timings for the 27th and 28th days of Ramazan for the main cities of Pakistan. According to the report, Islamabad's Fiqh-i-Hanafia followers will break the...

item.title: EU imposes sanctions on Chinese and Iranian companies for cyber attacks on member states
item.description: The European Union has officially imposed sanctions against two Chinese companies and one Iranian company due to their involvement in cyber attacks targeting EU member states. China-based Integrity Te...

item.title: Tanzania Adds Value to Minerals and Boosts Youth Employment in Mining Sector
item.description: Tanzania's mining sector is seeing solid results from reforms and expansion of local refineries, with minerals processed locally now valued at 5.802 trillion shillings. According to the Mining Commiss...

item.title: Gas Prices in Vancouver Reach Over $2 Per Litre Amid Global Supply Issues
item.description: Gas prices in Vancouver have exceeded $2 per litre, a figure much higher than the national average of approximately $1.64 per litre, according to the Canadian Automobile Association. Across British Co...

item.title: RBA Board Split Over Timing of Next Interest Rate Hike Amid Inflation Concerns
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) raised the cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.1 per cent, but the decision revealed a significant division among board members. Five voted for the hike while four op...

item.title: Suspension of Eight Opposition MPs in Lok Sabha Lifted After Consensus
item.description: On March 17, 2026, the Lok Sabha revoked the suspension of eight Opposition MPs through a voice vote, bringing an end to a political dispute that began earlier this year. The motion for revocation was...

item.title: Southern Alberta braces for unusually high temperatures this week
item.description: Southern Alberta is expected to experience record-breaking temperatures in the coming days, according to local meteorologists. The unusual warmth is attributed to a strong high-pressure system settlin...

item.title: Guiding Students to Develop Critical Thinking Skills in the Age of AI
item.description: In the evolving landscape of higher education, the rise of generative AI has transformed how students approach writing and research. A writing professor at Babson College describes the challenges and ...

item.title: Labour Introduces Programmes to Support Youth Employment Amid Rising Unemployment
item.description: Polly Toynbee reports from a London jobcentre, observing the struggles and aspirations of unemployed young people in the current economic climate. Many are highly educated and motivated, yet face inte...

item.title: Indian-origin Savani brothers convicted in US for multi-million dollar Medicaid and H-1B visa fraud schemes
item.description: Two Indian-origin brothers, Bhaskar Savani (60) and Arun Savani (58), have been convicted by a federal jury in Pennsylvania on 9 March 2026 for running a long-running racketeering conspiracy through t...

item.title: Understanding How Crude Oil is Refined into Petrol, Diesel, and Other Products
item.description: As tensions rise in the US–Israel conflict with Iran, global oil markets have felt the heat, especially due to disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for nearly 20% of the world's oil and ga...

item.title: Former Presidents Mbeki and Zuma Seek Review of Judge Khampepe’s Decision in TRC Inquiry
item.description: The Gauteng High Court is currently hearing a case brought by former South African presidents Thabo Mbeki and Jacob Zuma. Both leaders are challenging the decision of retired judge Sisi Khampepe, who ...

item.title: Adamson bounces back to defeat UP in UAAP softball rematch
item.description: In a highly anticipated rematch in UAAP Season 88 softball, the Adamson University softball team successfully avenged their first-round defeat against the University of the Philippines (UP). After a c...

item.title: Canadian Employers Adapt to Evolving Pay Transparency and AI Compliance Requirements
item.description: Canadian businesses are navigating a rapidly changing regulatory environment as both pay transparency and artificial intelligence (AI) disclosure laws take effect. According to the ADP survey 'Canada ...

item.title: Challenges of Bodaboda Safety and Traffic Compliance in Tanzania
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, the rapid growth of the bodaboda industry, despite being approved by the government as a public transport solution, has raised significant road safety concerns. Many riders frequentl...
